title: Set an automation across an entire account

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’re aware of automations and use them but they are only board by board.

Is there any way of setting an automation to operate on every board in the account?

For example, we use the automation for “Status=Done then Archive a pulse” but we want it to apply for every board, always, rather than having to set it every time we create a new board.

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: Great idea! Maybe also a way to set an automation and choose what boards it applies to. Obviously happy with an all or nothing to start though.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Maybe also a way to set an automation and choose what boards it applies to.

Excellent idea. +1

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
JohnW:

Maybe also a way to set an automation and choose what boards it applies to.

Yes this would be awesome!

posts.author: alex (Alex Parten)
posts.cookedHtml: While it doesn’t technically solve your problem, we have been using templates with all the automations we need built in to it. Then when we create a new board from the template, it’s ready to go! Something to think about. But certainly system-wide automations could be useful as well!

posts.author: Lobster (Ethyn Birch)
posts.cookedHtml: I have done the same thing. Built a template that has everything I need on it. The only problem is if you find something that you need across all of those already built templates, it doesn’t push out that change to all those boards you already created. This would be nice to have also.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
The only problem is if you find something that you need across all of those already built templates, it doesn’t push out that change to all those boards you already created.

Precisely. That’s the root of the issue here.

Like others here, we also set up template boards with automations built in. However, when we later add another automation, we have to separately add those new automations to all existing boards individually.

That’s a tedious and unnecessary step when Monday could offer 1) a global checkbox to apply to all boards in the account or b) checkboxes to separately select which Boards do and don’t get the automation.

I’d also like to see an account-wide report in the Automations Center showing which Boards have which automations applied. Even better if we could manage (add/deactivate/delete) automations applied to boards within the Automations Center via checkboxes.

posts.author: AlexisN (Alexis Nguyen)
posts.cookedHtml: I love the idea of a central point to manage all automations from

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone @JohnW, @Krishele @Eltjo @alex @Lobster! Great ideas here, for now using templates is the best way but know that we are working on more ways to universalize automations across the account but no ETA yet as we are doing this in stages. Stay tuned for more to come here though and keep giving us your ideas - we love them! Cheers!

posts.author: MaxCR86 (Max C. Reuveni)
posts.cookedHtml:
We ran into the same problem

We’re new to Monday, and are still figuring out what’s the best way for us to use the platform and it’s many great automations.

We set up boards, ended up deleting them and recreating them using a template that better suited our needs after the first couple of weeks of use, but now making new changes to that template requires us to recreate all of the boards in it’s image again (including the tedious step of migrating all the Pulses), or having to apply the change on each board separately.

This is relevant by the way not only to automations, but also to other column structures etc…

I would personally love for a template to be like a master slide - updating boards built in it’s image.

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml:
MaxCR86:

I would personally love for a template to be like a master slide - updating boards built in it’s image.

+1 to this idea @MaxCR86. Say when you saved the update to the template the next question would be tick the boards you want to update with the template updates.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
MaxCR86:

I would personally love for a template to be like a master slide

Must admit that’s how I thought it already worked when we signed up to Monday. Seems like obvious behaviour.

posts.author: blakeboris (Blake Boris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update on this? I’d love to be able to specify another board for the action of the automation

Edit: Nevermind, I found “When status changes to something, create an item in another board and link them using a link to item column in the selected board” which will work perfectly for me!

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Perfect! Let us know if you have any other questions @blakeboris

posts.author: becsweir (Rebecca Weir)
posts.cookedHtml: Please include this as standard functionality

posts.author: Elliot (Elliot Cryer)
posts.cookedHtml: This would save so much board upgrade/adjustment work. Please add this.

posts.author: matt.hart (Matt Hart)
posts.cookedHtml:
Incredibly helpful… We have 60+ boards at any given time. As our organization grows and optimizes, our automation needs change. Going through each board takes hours.

Someone else suggested having a central automations panel where you simply select which boards those settings apply to. This would great, even better, allow for multiple automation presets/collections for application to specific sets of boards.

posts.author: giangpham (Giang Pham)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be really helpful if we can have this. My team is importing projects from Basecamp to Monday and replicating automation from this board to another is such a tiring process.

posts.author: 33Harlow (Kris Harlow)
posts.cookedHtml: I echo the sentiment of this group, would save a TON of time!

posts.author: jelitots (Jo Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: We really need this @lauralev When’s it coming please?

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @jelitots - There is no firm ETA yet on when you will be able to set Automations across your account in one step. However, there is the ability already to set up Automations as templates: (58) PG Sales & Marketing —... which can save you considerable time. Save your most common automations and then quickly add them to new boards by scrolling to the very bottom of the automation library and adding with one click.

posts.author: s.wilson (SONYA WILSON)
posts.cookedHtml: This is urgent. It is such a nuisance to do this every time!

posts.author: baddogpaul (Paul Cotter)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be so useful - Come On Monday - help us out!

posts.author: willi (Willi Cohen)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update here? There are so many tedious issues with Monday that could SO easily be fixed

posts.author: gperrine (Gregory Perrine)
posts.cookedHtml: Trying to follow this thread, has this solution been developed yet?

posts.author: kylechlopcik (Kyle Chlopcik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Monday, any possibility for this central automations point to happen? We just started managing projects in Monday and have already over 50 WFs across different boards and many more to come.

It would be really helpful to have them all in once place and easily accessable.

posts.author: JohnTMF (John)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday.com Team, is there an update on this yet? read through the several threads and saw there was supposed to be a release in 2022?

Aside the copy and pasting automation workflows, would indeed be nice to have a better Workflow automation interface as it’s almost impossible to see the details each rule that was set up.

Thanks!

posts.author: DanielR (Daniel Reed)
posts.cookedHtml:
Chipping my voice in here, this is a very highly needed feature. We manage 50+ projects with each project as a board that’s created by a template with automations pre-created. That works great until we decide we want to update the template… and have to manually re-create the automation 50 times.

This, or a revival of the old “Master Board” concept for dynamic template linking are very high priority for us.

posts.author: MattDaillyxwhy (Matt Dailly)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

Has there been any progress on this?

We currently handle many different projects which we have boards for that can be duplicated and copied across to a new project folder whenever a new project comes up.

I currently have 4 folders, each containing 10 boards, and 47 different automations per board. With no way to automatically save automations with specific details i.e. notify notes, or associated person, or save board automations as an “automation set” or “automation templates keeping information” I will manually have to create 1880 automations manually. Is there no way anyone knows of of how to keep a list of automations holding the information that can be easily replicated into a new board when created?

I can save the automations I need as a template, but these templates are blank and need to be populated with information including notify text, corresponding persons, status’s etc. which takes and incredibly long time.

Thanks,
Matt

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @JohnW and the rest of our community!

Thanks for taking the time to share your suggestion. Great minds think alike – our development team is already working on allowing customers to set automation across an entire account. This feature is planned be part of the new workflow builder.

Requests like yours help guide us in prioritizing the features that our customers care about the most. So keep the ideas coming because your feedback impacts hundreds of thousands monday.com customers who will enjoy this setting automations across accounts because of you.

Have another idea? Submit it here

posts.author: TIMS-Crystal (Crystal Hopper)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’re going to implement an automation across many boards that does the exact same thing. I would like to create the automation once and then be able to copy, move or somehow deploy it to many other boards.

To do this I might create the automation once, then select it (like you select a pulse on a board), and get the wysiwyg that allows me to move or copy something.

Perhaps as an admin I might also have a view of all automations in my Monday.com account and be able from an admin view to choose an automation and copy, move, delete them.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
TIMS-Crystal:

Perhaps as an admin I might also have a view of all automations in my Monday.com account and be able from an admin view to choose an automation and copy, move, delete them.

@TIMS-Crystal Agree.

Feel free to add your vote to this similar existing feature request:
https://community.monday.com/t/any-way-to-set-an-automation-across-an-entire-account

posts.author: tomsvision (Tom Guy)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have several project boards in different folders with the same column names. When I implement a new automation I really need to be able to copy that to other boards.

Tom

posts.author: Elliot (Elliot Cryer)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be the best. Along with copying columns. I have to remake so many columns and automation’s just to try out new work flows. It’s so repetitive.

posts.author: zach-xpndinteractive (Zachary)
posts.cookedHtml: This is necessary if you want to work with bigger companies. I can’t be bothered to create 50 different automations over 100 times on different boards.

posts.author: ivanhong-monday.com (ivan-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all!

We can definitely hear where you’re coming from and I’ve already gone ahead and passed this feedback along to our Autopilot Team regarding this! It definitely sounds like a powerful feature that could benefit many teams using our platform!

posts.author: jelitots (Jo Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @ivanhong-monday.com Has there been any update on this? We would really like to copy automations across boards to save time.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 for this feature request.

We are working with over 300 boards were-off probably 75% are created via a template. I am aiming to have a similar workflow in those 75%, but with a slight change due to a new feature from Monday or an internal work-process change I find myself updating a lot of boards which costs me way too much time.

Because of this small differences between boards start to happen and differences in work-processes start to happen as well; in one board you do get a notification when status change to Immediate, in the other you don’t. In one board you will be unassigned when status change into Done, in the other you don’t. In one board there is an extra status column in the other there isn’t. This is very confusing for team members if you are trying to get into the same workflow.

Therefor I am seeking for any timesaving feature to be able to keep the boards as much as the same as possible. This feature would help a lot for that.
Another feature request which I think will be a perfect supplement on this one is Set Board as MASTER Board (Column Structure, Permissions, and Automations Applied Across Linked Boards). This wold let you adda new automation to the MASTER board and it would add it to all the connected boards. This will keep all the boards the same and would make sure you don’t have to manually copy paste the new automation into a lot of other boards. What do you think?

posts.author: Sotiris (bootstraps)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

It would be so useful to be able to save a bunch of automations and copy them in another similar bord.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Centralized template management is coming later in Q1 for Enterprise accounts:

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the update @PolishedGeek. This is great news since it is a highly wanted feature and almost a musthave. It’s a shame it is on enterprise though.

posts.author: bholt (Brian Holt)
posts.cookedHtml: Having a master board like you explained would be very helpful and is a basic requirement.

posts.author: KColosiPCCI (Kristen Colosi)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you know when in 2022 this is expected to be released?

posts.author: Domyx (Kris)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a must have feature in my opinion.

There is a save a template option, but the problem is that default automations cannot be saved as templates which makes it confusing to understand which automations I have on the new board and it does not save the data input in these automations.

It’s just giant time waste basically. Please add it for Pro plan at least.

posts.author: matiaspulido (Matias Pulido)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed, this feature is urgently needed.

posts.author: DGSampo (Daniel Sampson)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 from me on this too - desperately need this.

posts.author: Francisco (Francisco Almeida)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 This is a must have feature in my opinion.

posts.author: Olga_M (Olga)
posts.cookedHtml: Very need this feature, thanks!

posts.author: wfe2410 (William Estes)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update on this?

posts.author: elosrebe (Paul Beets)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on this feature! We aren’t very big … we have 5 boards. We can’t be copying automations by hand to each and every board.

Really hope this feature isn’t stuck in enterprise!!!

posts.author: DanielR (Daniel Reed)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on this feature as well… having to recreate an automation across 50+ boards is a non-starter.

We need to be able to copy/paste from one board to another, or have enhanced Templates that incorporate prior selections, or a batch script to deploy to multiple boards… something!

The Master Template function would be positively amazing… any updates on that, is it still on the product roadmap?

posts.author: ProdUser (Bill Ulammandakh)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this feature? This is desperately needed. Do I really have to manually recreate the same automation across all the dozens of boards we have? Saving an automation as a template doesn’t seem to save all the details of the automation, but only the skeleton of the automation.

posts.author: john222 (John D. )
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this release? A general timeline would be greatly appreciated - or alternatively a road map with this feature listed so we may track. Thanks

posts.author: bmaggetti (Brad Maggetti)
posts.cookedHtml:
New to monday and as I build our boards AND tackle the set of problems around introducing monday.com to everyone in our organization to build their confidence around it so they actually want to use it…little inevitable mistakes in automations from manually copying from one board to another feels like it could be a deal breaker for us.

Need to be able to update automations for many boards simultaneously to keep consistency with updated workflows or I fear I’m going to have trouble convincing the organization this was a good idea…

posts.author: paul-enoda (PaFi)
posts.cookedHtml: To get around the slow BS from Monday.com I created a “template” board that I manipulate all of the automations and structure. I then duplicate this board and move groups/items from a board to the newly created board to keep all automations the same. This is stupid and a complete time waste. Also, the automations view is the WORST! It is almost impossible to see what I have created and how everything works together. Get rid of the cutesy UI crap and just provide a better list and throw in a workflow viewer.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Crystal, Thanks for taking the time to share your suggestion. Great minds think alike – our development team is already working on allowing customers to set automation across an entire account. This feature is planned be part of the new workflow builder.

Requests like yours help guide us in prioritizing the features that our customers care about the most. So keep the ideas coming because your feedback impacts hundreds of thousands monday.com customers who will enjoy copying automations from one board to another because of you.

Have another idea? Submit it here.

posts.author: leonidas (Leonidas)
posts.cookedHtml: Any news on this feature? It would really save a lot of time and mistakes when creating, editing and deleting automatons.

posts.author: tiagarmonsway (Tia Garmonsway)
posts.cookedHtml: Even being able to simply duplicate an automation in another board would be beneficial. Having to reinput an automation every time we need it is incredibly cumbersome.

posts.author: Wh1skeyJack (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml:
I second this, any word on when we will be able to see this option?
Thanks so much!
~Mike

posts.author: Hayley_Hill (Hayley Hill)
posts.cookedHtml: What is the update on this? I believe it’s still not possible to set an automation across an entire account and it looks like this is fairly old now.

posts.author: denverrock89 (Matthew Steadman)
posts.cookedHtml: Has this still not been implemented for 4 years? It’d be great to get this implemented to cut down on hours of time for people with many boards and workspaces. Just curious if there’s a timeline for completion on the Monday side?

posts.author: Hayley_Hill (Hayley Hill)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Juliette - is there a rough ETA on when this will be available? I have found several threads on the community asking for the same thing, going back SEVERAL years. I note that a release was done this month which has small changes that are not going to make anywhere near the same impact as this feature would (I don’t even use WorkForms). And you don’t actually give any forward view on future releases/development. Why are you not giving your customers what they are actually asking for??

posts.author: Jappa (Jasper Schulten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Any update? ETA? position on Roadmap?

posts.author: Sotiris (bootstraps)
posts.cookedHtml:
great! any new regarding the release?
Thanks

posts.author: VETSmarketing (Marketing)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this feature?

posts.author: cserra (Chris Serra)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates to this feature or am I missing it? I have a lot of automations I am currently setting up in a template for future boards but I’d like them to be able to automatically deploy to currently open boards.

posts.author: maalfunction (Beatriz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Checking for an update on this as well… It really takes most of my “Monday time” to have to replicate the automations from my Template throughout the other boards…

Also, same would apply to the Dashboard Filters. Everytime I add a new board to the Dashboard, it comes from scratch; I would like to see an option that I can turn Default into my filters to be applied to all new boards added.

posts.author: Micke (Micke)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you know if this has been added yet?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Update: This feature is almost done being developed and we hope to be able to share it soon! Currently, with monday Workflows, you are able to edit and move items from different boards in the same workflow. In the next few months, monday workflows will also allow users to create automation with account-level triggers (e.g. “when a user joins the account”).

To be the first to hear updates about this feature, make sure to turn notifications. How to turn on notifications for a post

posts.author: dbcgroup (Alex Rosenberg)
posts.cookedHtml: Will this be available for all plans? Thank you.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! I just spoke with the automation team, and they are in the final stages of developing two different features that will impact account wide automations. Here are the updates:

  • The “when user joined the account” trigger block will start being released in the next few weeks
  • The ability to set account level automations - Will be available with monday workflows.

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community! Thank you for sharing your feedback about creating automations that work across an entire account. While the ability to run automations on all boards or select specific boards isn’t currently on our roadmap, we’d love to hear more about your use cases for this request - it helps us better understand the need and prioritize it for the future.

In the meantime, we wanted to share that we’ve recently released a new account-level trigger in monday workflows (Enterprise only): “When user joins account.” You can find more details about this feature here.

We truly value your feedback and look forward to building features that meet your needs!

posts.author: J.Neris (Jason Neris)
posts.cookedHtml: What happened! You got so close to helping us out. We want this feature badly.

posts.author: TDGregory (Tabetha)
posts.cookedHtml: Currently, I have some automations that are on all of my boards across the enterprise. It would be super helpful to be able to manage these automations on the enterprise level rather than the board level.

posts.author: Kmcew (Kerin McEwing)
posts.cookedHtml: So so close to a solution for this. Now we have to continue to set up and manage duplicated workflows for each team we roll out to, rather than one single workflow for the organisation. This is marked as “coming soon” so you may want to change that.
